races.race_name: Pontefract Races Autism In Racing EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Clash Of Hearts
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a 6f Group 3 winner; third of 15 (subsequently promoted to second) in York novice (6f, good) on debut in May and again showed considerable promise when third in quite valuable conditions race at Pontefract (5f, good to firm) a fortnight later; back up in trip today; ought to be a tough nut to crack.
runners.comment: Launched career with two very promising runs last month (6f/5f); should be tough to beat

runners.name: Regal Tiger
runners.spotlight: Weakened into well-held fourth of six on last month's debut at Haydock (6f, soft) but was only 3-1 for that and this well-bred colt might be seen to better effect on the faster ground here.
runners.comment: Weakened into well-held fourth on debut but may fare better on the faster ground here

runners.name: Onslaught
runners.spotlight: 68,000gns yearling; second foal; dam French maiden, half-sister to winners Al Tariq (UAE 6f Group 3) and Lehaim (useful 5f/5.5f); has good standard to reach on debut.
runners.comment: 68,000gns yearling out of half-sister to a UAE 6f Group 3 winner

runners.name: Excessive
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful and well-connected 6f 2yo winner; outran 66-1 odds when fourth of eight on recent debut at Carlisle (5f, good to soft); that was probably just a run-of-the-mill contest but he's open to improvement over this longer trip and with that experience under his belt.
runners.comment: Outran 66-1 odds when fourth over 5f at Carlisle recently; can improve

runners.name: La Fuerza
runners.spotlight: First foal of French Listed-placed 7f winner (RPR 105); with a good stable but finished down the field when 28-1 for 6f Nottingham novice 18 days ago and needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: With good yard but finished down the field when 28-1 for 6f Nottingham novice 18 days ago

runners.name: Sole Ruler
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to prolific 5f/7f winner Elegant Erin (including 2yo; RPR 98); towards rear throughout when 50-1 for debut at Ripon (5f, good) 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Towards rear throughout when 50-1 for debut at Ripon (5f) 18 days ago

races.race_name: Anna Woolley Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: In-form filly who prevailed by half a length at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) most recently, opening her account at the 16th attempt; back up 3lb but is still unexposed at this trip on turf; following up is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Opened her account in Doncaster event last time; still unexposed over 6f on turf

runners.name: Ideal Guest
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly last time, failing to back up reappearance effort and continuing his long losing spell; otherwise well capable of taking a serious hand off current mark, provided he rebounds.
runners.comment: Capable of taking a serious hand off current mark, provided he rebounds

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: Reasonably solid on 2026 form that features a success in early May and close third last time, both at Redcar; won off current mark over C&D (good to firm) last June; likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: Reasonably solid on 2026 form; won off current mark over C&D last June; likely player

runners.name: Instant Bond
runners.spotlight: Contrasting performances since returned to turf from AW, winning narrowly at Leicester then trailing home last (albeit something was possibly amiss) at Doncaster; a possible player if back on song.
runners.comment: Scored narrowly at Leicester returned to turf; something was possibly amiss last time
runners.owner: David Tate, Danny Lawson & Kevin Broadley

runners.name: Diligent Henry
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins over 7f on Wolverhampton AW; not particularly solid on turf form or on overall 2026 results; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has gained both wins over 7f on Wolverhampton AW

runners.name: Lindoro
runners.spotlight: Both efforts this term fail to match the promise shown in two outings last July; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces in this second handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Chance largely depends on the effects of tongue-tie and cheekpieces

runners.name: Ishe Worth Agamble
runners.spotlight: Scored three times off lower marks in May-July last term (ended up with a peak mark 7lb higher than current one); generally consistent since, running-on third at Nottingham (6f, good) most recently; in the mix.
runners.comment: Scored three times last term; largely consistent since, running-on third latest start

runners.name: Ziva's Star
runners.spotlight: Proved resurgent in first-time cheekpieces at Leicester (classified race) most recently but has her limitations and remains a maiden.
runners.comment: Proved resurgent in cheekpieces last time but has her limitations

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Back in better form (over 5f/5.5f) this month, winning AW classified event then solid second (behind a rival who was on the middle leg of hat-trick) in Wetherby handicap; has won over 6f; 0-14 on turf but holds a fighting chance.
runners.comment: Record of 0-14 on turf but he's in form and posted a solid effort at Wetherby last time

runners.name: Maui Breeze
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on her 7f-1m2f form for current stable but this drop back to 6f is an angle in with her; recorded sole success at trip, albeit in an ordinary AW maiden.
runners.comment: Drop back to 6f is the angle with her; recorded sole success at this trip
runners.owner: H S Hutchinson And W Glass

runners.name: Invincible Ruby
runners.spotlight: Hinted at a revival on penultimate start and comfortably took advantage of a reduced mark at Carlisle (6f, good) last Monday; even with 5lb penalty she's still favourably treated on peak form; respected, assuming the widest draw doesn't prove a hindrance.
runners.comment: Even with 5lb penalty for Carlisle win she remains well treated on peak form; respected

runners.name: So Grateful
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts since his last win and failed to beat a rival on seasonal debut; best watched unless the betting suggests a revival is likely.
runners.comment: Out of sorts since last win

races.race_name: Ponte Premiership Points Mean Prizes Handicap

runners.name: Lethal Nymph
runners.spotlight: Perhaps best over 5f nowadays and couldn't quite last home in big field over 6f at York last weekend (still ran respectably); gained last turf success off current mark; possibilities.
runners.comment: Couldn't quite last home over 6f last weekend; gained last turf win off current mark

runners.name: Goyard
runners.spotlight: Completed a Ripon double last summer prior to showing no progress in two starts upon entering handicaps; unlikely to defy top weight on seasonal debut unless he comes back better as a 4yo.
runners.comment: Unlikely to defy top weight on reappearance unless he comes back better as a 4yo

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times this season, including when favourite at Catterick last time; back up in grade but he's 3lb below last winning mark and seems to have a fighting chance.
runners.comment: Placed a few times this term, including last time; 3lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Ventura Express
runners.spotlight: On a near two-year losing spell but he's a four-time C&D winner and can't be dismissed off his reduced mark; made the frame in last two starts, latest back at Pontefract.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner who can't be dismissed off his reduced mark

runners.name: D Flawless
runners.spotlight: Fitted with hood for last two starts, winning on AW then respectable fourth at Pontefract, both over 6f; placed over 5f once; 0-5 on turf but could go well in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Fitted with hood for last two starts, winning on AW then fourth at Pontefract

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: Seems to be coming steadily back to form, fourth at Hamilton most recently; 10lb below last winning mark and looks a possible danger assuming the resurgence continues.
runners.comment: Seems to be coming steadily back to form; 10lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Dakota Gold
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner down the years and formerly a smart performer but has been largely out of sorts since last success; failed to beat a rival on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Formerly smart but has been largely out of sorts since last success

runners.name: Hiya Maite
runners.spotlight: Absent since running respectably when favourite for AW contest in January; on a two-year losing spell but is attractively treated off his lower mark back on turf; further market support should be noted.
runners.comment: On a two-year losing spell but is attractively treated off lower mark back on turf

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: Best effort this term when close second at Hamilton on penultimate start; has a record over 4-7 over C&D and is 3lb below last winning mark; interesting returned to this scenario.
runners.comment: Has a record of 4-7 over C&D and is 3lb below last winning mark; interesting back here

runners.name: Squealer
runners.spotlight: Has a poor strike-rate over the last three years and has proved inconsistent since last success (off 12lb higher); chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Inconsistent since last win; chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear

races.race_name: Pontefract Castle Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Orionis
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in Class 3 handicap at Goodwood (1m4f) on seasonal debut, making it four wins from her last seven starts and breaking into triple-digit RPR territory; this improving filly figures highly in calculations.
runners.comment: Prevailed in Class 3 handicap at Goodwood on seasonal debut; improving filly

runners.name: Coedana
runners.spotlight: Has posted good efforts in 1m4f Listed event at Goodwood and 1m3f Group 3 at Carlisle this term, maintaining her solid record and steadily progressive RPRs; new rating gives her a leading chance in this field.
runners.comment: Good efforts in Listed and Group 3 races this term, maintaining her progressive form
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Revoir
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-8 since debut win but her Listed record reads 25222, beaten only half a length at Haydock (10.5f) on seasonal debut most recently; stays 1m4f; yard has an excellent record in this race; fighting chance.
runners.comment: Listed record reads 25222; yard has an excellent record in this race; fighting chance

runners.name: Merveilleux Lapin
runners.spotlight: Ex-Polish filly who is a Listed winner; unlucky second in Windsor handicap (1m2f) on stable/seasonal debut and is plausibly open to further progress back up in trip; won her last 1m4f attempt; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ex-Polish filly; unlucky second in Windsor handicap on stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Brielle
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Ryka; has registered her handicap wins in Class 4, latest at Thirsk (1m4f) on reappearance when fitted with first-time tongue-tie (retained today); faces a stiffer assignment back up sharply in grade.
runners.comment: Scored in first-time tongue-tie on reappearance but this is a stiffer assignment

runners.name: Lemsairbat
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-13 for current yard but her 2026 Listed form (over shorter) is encouraging, including as regards this step back up to 1m4f; stable had a great week at Royal Ascot; this may be the time to catch her.
runners.comment: Stable had a great week at Royal Ascot; this may be the time to catch her
runners.owner: H R D McCalmont & Hamish McCalmont & Andrew N C Bengough

runners.name: Little Dorrit
runners.spotlight: Showed progress in her first season last year until taking a step backwards in Group 3 on final start; ran well in sole Listed attempt; raced only five times and may do better still; interesting on reappearance.
runners.comment: Raced only five times and may do better still; interesting on reappearance

runners.name: Ghaiyya
runners.spotlight: Has developed into quite a useful handicapper and her record this season is solid, winning on reappearance then making the frame twice, most recently in Class 2 at Carlisle; this is harder, however.
runners.comment: Solid record in handicaps this term; faces a harder task on these terms

runners.name: Ryka
runners.spotlight: Below par in similar event at Goodwood in sole start this term (generally progressive otherwise) and is again the only runner lumbered with a penalty, incurred due to a Listed win that was gained on French soil.
runners.comment: Below par in sole start this term and is again the only runner lumbered with a penalty

runners.name: Cabrera
runners.spotlight: Has fairly useful figures but an underwhelming strike-rate and this is the most difficult task of her career (has the worst chance at the weights); fifth in the Thirsk handicap won by Brielle most recently.
runners.comment: Has an underwhelming strike-rate and this is the most difficult task of her career
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 1 And Mrs David Egan

races.race_name: Moor Top Farm Shop Hemsworth Pontefract Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Trojan Soldier
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; seconds were becoming a habit before the cheekpieces went on and he won at Southwell (1m6f, AW), Ripon (2m, good) and Pontefract (2m1f, good to soft) on last three outings; latest (after five days off) saw him assert by about 4l in the final 1f and the four-timer may well be beckoning despite 5lb rise.
runners.comment: 3-3 in cheekpieces, asserting in good style over 2m1f here latest; major chance again

runners.name: Anzac Day
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Flat winner (soft) but has made the track only five times since that success in October 2023 and was right out of form on last two outings last year; changed hands and left Andrew Balding before well beaten in a maiden hurdle one month ago; a recent positive sign is lacking.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a maiden hurdle one month ago for new yard; recent positive sign is lacking

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; upped to 2m when making all at Ripon in April in his second handicap; placed since over that trip a Haydock and Thirsk and it looks as if today's extra 2f may well be an asset; major player.
runners.comment: Moving up to 2m has worked well and it looks as if today's extra 2f may well be an asset

runners.name: Secret Force
runners.spotlight: On his fourth trainer; 1m3f Flat and 2m hurdle winner in Ireland for Gavin Cromwell last autumn; on the premises recently for new yard at Hamilton (1m3f, good) and York (1m4f amateurs, good); cheekpieces (worn on two of last three starts) are replaced with a visor; staying trips on the Flat may now be worth exploring, so he needs a second look.
runners.comment: On the premises for new yard over 1m3f and 1m4f; Flat staying trips may be worth exploring

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: Sole Flat win (31 attempts) was debut in 2020; had two hurdle wins (2m5f and 2m4f) in 2025 and there have been bits of chase form in recent months; visor returning instead of blinkers might possibly help, but there are more likely candidates.
runners.comment: Sole Flat win (31 attempts) was debut in 2020; had two hurdle wins (2m5f and 2m4f) in 2025

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Very busy and he's had his share of wins, including four over 2m in 2025, but that's been mostly on AW which has dominated his career; best turf ratings two and three runs back before tailed off (might have overdone it up front) at Goodwood latest; again has just four rivals today but others are preferred.
runners.comment: AW has dominated his career and he tailed off on latest start

races.race_name: Sunpak Potatoes Handicap

runners.name: Azure Zain
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in big field at Ascot (6f, good to firm) on return from break, opening his turf account; rested a further six weeks since, keeping him fresh; one of two last-time winners for his yard in this field; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Prevailed narrowly in big field at Ascot to open his turf account; again runs fresh

runners.name: Ray Mon Dough
runners.spotlight: Front-running, thrice-raced colt whose 6f form comprises a ready win on debut and clear second last time, both at Lingfield (AW/turf); plausibly still open to progress; tongue-tie fitted on handicap debut; respected.
runners.comment: Front-running, three-raced colt who is plausibly still open to progress; respected

runners.name: Rock Opera
runners.spotlight: Belatedly regained the winning thread in C&D event (good) last time, perhaps benefiting from the removal of visor and beating a subsequent scorer (pair clear); still favourably treated even though he's gone back up 5lb; strong contender.
runners.comment: Suited by removal of visor in C&D race last time, beating a subsequent scorer (pair clear)

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Form at Pontefract features two wins last term and a close second during a consistent spell this spring; however, finished almost 6l behind Rock Opera here last time and has work to do to reverse those placings.
runners.comment: Suited by Pontefract but is held by Rock Opera on course running last time

runners.name: Badri
runners.spotlight: Much the oldest contestant in this field, being a 9yo, and he's conceding weight all round; managed to defy top weight at Redcar on penultimate start but that was in a slightly lower grade.
runners.comment: Oldest contender; defied top weight on penultimate start but that was in a lower grade

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Listed/Group races in 2024; on a three-year losing spell but his Class 2 form this term for new yard includes a couple of encouraging efforts; possibilities off current mark back down in grade.
runners.comment: On a three-year lost spell but has possibilities off current mark back down in grade

runners.name: Mister Sox
runners.spotlight: Comfortably beat Rock Opera over C&D in spring 2025, albeit was in better form at the time, that success the middle leg of a hat-trick; not solid on 2026 results but has slipped to an attractive mark.
runners.comment: Comfortably beat Rock Opera over C&D in spring 2025, albeit was in better form at the time

races.race_name: Next Family Day Sunday 26th July Handicap

runners.name: Keep An Eye On It
runners.spotlight: Didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages when beaten a short head on handicap debut at Southwell (7f, AW) in April; hung right in the closing stages when good third of ten at Musselburgh (1m, good) ten days later; absent ten weeks since and, after five races, may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Placed both handicaps; off ten weeks since and, after five races, may have more to offer

runners.name: Ravishing Beauty
runners.spotlight: Three wins overall include a comfortable C&D success last July on her only race at Pontefract; back near her best when winning at Carlisle (1m, soft; probably fine on good to firm) 13 days ago and looks a leading contender despite going back up 3lb.
runners.comment: 1-1 at Pontefract; back near her best when winning at Carlisle (1m) 13 days ago

runners.name: Masaban
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dermot Weld; 8-11 for his handicap debut in seven-runner race at Ripon (1m, good) last month and won easily from the front; 8lb higher when respectable third of 14 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) eight days later, having chased the leaders, and needs to resume improvement with another 2lb rise today; he's had just five races, so it may be possible.
runners.comment: Odds-on in April for easy win and respectable 3rd in his first two handicaps; off since

runners.name: Kiss For An Angel
runners.spotlight: Close second of five at Redcar (1m, good) last November in his sole nursery; however, beaten a long way on both starts this season (1m2f/1m) and that puts him well down the list.
runners.comment: Close second of five in November (1m) but beaten a long way on both starts this season

runners.name: Gennadius
runners.spotlight: Sole win from his 20 races was an AW maiden in January; has a form chance judged on peak efforts (turf and AW) but he was comfortably held on last three outings (gelded before latest) and does not look the percentage call.
runners.comment: Sole win from his 20 races was an AW maiden in January; gelded before latest outing

runners.name: Thats My Boy Luke
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m handicaps last summer at Ripon, from 25 races overall; backward steps in his three starts this season leave him 3lb below last winning mark but with plenty to prove currently.
runners.comment: Backward steps in his three starts this season leave him 3lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins last August but made the frame on last three outings; carries his head high, including in a duel at Musselburgh last time, and cheekpieces are enlisted today.
runners.comment: 2 wins last August; in the frame last three starts; carries his head high; cheekpieces now

runners.name: Mount King
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come over C&D (from 11 visits to Pontefract) and latest was off 3lb higher last July; he therefore cannot be comfortably dismissed despite being well held on all three starts (two here) this term.
runners.comment: Three of his four wins were C&D and latest was off 3lb higher last July; well held in 2026

races.race_name: L & R Pest Control Services Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Ten wins over hurdles, including over C&D; in front when unseating rider two out in race won by Snapaudaciaheros here eight days ago; a possible
runners.comment: Ten wins over hurdles; in front when unseating rider two out here last time; a possible
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Bouboule
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; hasn't been in best of form on the Flat and has plenty to find on his latest run over hurdles.
runners.comment: 0-8; hasn't been in top form on the Flat and plenty to find on latest hurdles run

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Four places from ten runs over hurdles; ran creditably in handicaps on last three starts in this sphere (good to soft/good) and he was a good second on the Flat at Ayr (1m2f, soft) on Friday; chance.
runners.comment: Chance on recent hurdles form; good second on Flat at Ayr (1m2f, soft) on Friday

runners.name: Huit Reflets
runners.spotlight: Two wins over hurdles, the first in this race in 2024; 22l fourth in this race last year; went chasing later on last season, winning at Sedgefield on Boxing Day; now back hurdling after a break.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024 (23l fourth last year); has since been chasing; back after a break

runners.name: Knockeranna Rose
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden over hurdles; well beaten last time at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) having been fourth over C&D on his previous start; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-8; well beaten at Perth last time (good to soft) but fair fourth here on previous start

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: Just 1-23 over hurdles but was well clear of the rest when 0.5l second to Snapaudaciaheros (2lb worse off) over C&D eight days ago; good chance.
runners.comment: 1-23; clear second to Superaudaciaheros over C&D eight days ago (hampered); chance

runners.name: Theirshegoes
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since November 2024; well beaten over 2m5f at Kelso last time but comes into the reckoning on her previous close-up fifth to Snapaudaciaheros over C&D in April (good).
runners.comment: Last win came in 2024, but close fifth over C&D two runs ago; comes into the reckoning

runners.name: Snapaudaciaheros
runners.spotlight: Has won two of her last three races (pulled up on soft ground in between), the latest over C&D eight days ago (good) when a few of today's rivals were behind her; up 6lb; should go well again.
runners.comment: Has won two of her last three races, latest here 8 days ago; up 6lb; should go well again

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; off the mark on fifth hurdle start in a novice handicap at Perth two weeks ago (2m, good); stiffer task off 7lb higher mark, but is progressive.
runners.comment: Progressive; off the mark at Perth two weeks ago; stiffer task off 7lb higher; a possible

runners.name: Top Flight Century
runners.spotlight: Ex-Adam Nicol; winner at Sedgefield on stable debut in December (2m4f, good to soft) but below par since, including on the Flat (AW) last time.
runners.comment: Sedgefield winner on Boxing Day (2m4f); below par since including on the Flat last time

runners.name: Curious Mrs Fox
runners.spotlight: Unexposed as a hurdler, but was 26l behind Superaudaciaheros over C&D eight days ago (good; 100-1).
runners.comment: Unexposed; beaten 26l by Superaudacaiaheros over C&D eight days ago (100-1)

races.race_name: Chris Lowther & Brian Ellison 28 Years Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Getaway Vic
runners.spotlight: Point/dual bumper winner who launched hurdling career with three good efforts, the latest a second at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) on Friday; has another big role to play if he makes a quick reappearance here.
runners.comment: Point/dual bumper winner who launched hurdling career with three good efforts; big player

runners.name: Loman Lady
runners.spotlight: Joined Dan Skelton after last month's Irish point second and comfortably made winning rules debut in good-ground course bumper eight days ago, having made smooth headway from the rear; obvious contender on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Impressed when winning course bumper on rules debut eight days ago; obvious contender

runners.name: Millena Agent
runners.spotlight: Won a heavy-ground point here in November but that was her only success from 11 starts in that sphere and she was well held when third of six over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) last month; improvement required.
runners.comment: Won a point here in November but was well held when third on recent hurdle debut

runners.name: Beyond The Verge
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground Kelso bumper on debut in September 2024; absent since safely held in stronger bumper at Ayr in April 2025 but should have a future over hurdles; market helpful.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; makes hurdle debut after 435-day absence; market helpful

runners.name: Rachel Lopez
runners.spotlight: No threat to 1-18 favourite when second here (2m, good to soft) on last month's stable/hurdle debut and well beaten when last of three over C&D good) ten days later.
runners.comment: Well beaten when last of three over C&D last month; up against it again today

runners.name: Dolly Dior
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in her two bumpers but struggled when 50-1 for hurdle debut at Perth (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Struggled when 50-1 for hurdle debut at Perth (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago

runners.name: Fortunes Dawn
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who was about 35l behind Getaway Vic on last month's rules debut at Cartmel (2m6f, good); look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer who was about 35l behind Getaway Vic on last month's rules debut

runners.name: Frisky Whiskey
runners.spotlight: Made underwhelming hurdles debut when well-held sixth at Market Rasen (2m2f, good) last month and needs new tongue-tie to enable considerable improvement if the sponsors are to win their own race.
runners.comment: Made underwhelming hurdles debut when well-held sixth at Market Rasen (2m2f) last month

runners.name: Run Happy
runners.spotlight: Made quite encouraging rules debut when fifth of 14 in pointers' bumper at Aintree (good) last month but was pulled up in maiden hurdle at Southwell (2m4f, good) 12 day ago.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in pointers' bumper last month but pulled up on recent hurdle debut
runners.owner: The Hon A Vestey, Sangwin & J Scutt

runners.name: Teofimo
runners.spotlight: Showed significant ability on the Flat and over hurdles for Jim Bolger in Ireland but started off for current stable with two disappointing hurdle runs last summer (2m/2m4f; had wind operation afterwards); returns from 343-day absence with tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: Showed promise in Ireland but absent since two disappointing stable runs last summer

runners.name: Redbridge Rocco
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in Irish points (fell four times) and absent since making low-key rules debut in 2m4f maiden hurdle at Perth (good) last summer.
runners.comment: Absent since making low-key rules debut in 2m4f maiden hurdle at Perth (good) last summer

races.race_name: Matfen Hall Hotel Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cobbler's Boy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark as a chaser at Newton Abbot (2m, good) last month, easily beating two rivals; raced too freely and succumbed to a stronger finisher when odds-on to follow up over hurdles there a week later but probably still has untapped potential over fences if he settles better here.
runners.comment: Easily beat two rivals to open chasing account last month; still has potential over fences

runners.name: Conquer The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Started off for Adam Nicol with two comfortable C&D wins last month (good to soft/good) and travelled strongly for a long way before failing to see out 2m4f here a fortnight ago; upped significantly in grade today but this drop back in trip is the obvious move, and he might still have more to offer for his new stable.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner for new stable in May; stretched by 2m4f last time but still ran well

runners.name: Glory And Honour
runners.spotlight: Won this race off today's mark a year ago (good; first-time blinkers) and reverts to chasing after hard-fought hurdle win here (2m, good; refitted blinkers) a fortnight ago; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2025 and reverts to fences after hard-fought course hurdle win this month

runners.name: Kientzheim
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in French bumpers and 2-8 in British hurdles; ran big race from the front when third of ten at Cartmel (2m1f, good) last month and could take some catching if she gets into a good rhythm on this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Ran well from the front over hurdles last month; dangerous if she takes well to chasing

runners.name: Parisencore
runners.spotlight: Weakened out of contention at Aintree last month but this race is a bit easier and he's now only 3lb higher than when keeping on well to beat Glory And Honour at Newcastle (2m4f, good) in April; also suited by 2m and might be aided by recent wind surgery; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Safely held at Aintree last time but beat Glory And Honour at Newcastle in April; possible
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

runners.name: Herja
runners.spotlight: Just 1-10 over hurdles and yet fully to convince after three chasing starts; only third best on merit and no threat to winner when second at Bangor (2m4f, good) last month; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Only third best on merit and not threat to winner when second at Bangor (2m4f) last month

runners.name: Cossack Chach
runners.spotlight: Seen only one since winning Sligo beginners' chase (2m1f, good) for Joseph O'Brien in October 2024, when pulled up in Haydock handicap for James Moffatt in November; tricky to weigh up on debut for latest new stable but was given a wind operation in January and market support will pique interest.
runners.comment: Absent since pulled up in November but worth glance in betting before stable debut

races.race_name: Jethro's Cafe Of Haltwhistle Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: Bidding for a hat-trick of hurdle wins having won his last two races over 2m4f here, last time beating Well Educated by 9l; won over 1m5f on the Flat at Hamilton in between (good); 9lb higher than last win, but a chance despite dropping back in trip.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick after two 2m4f wins here (Flat winner in between); up 9lb; chance
runners.owner: J Monkhouse R De Robeck A Miller

runners.name: Well Educated
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024, but has run plenty of good races since; runner-up here on all three starts this season (2m/2m4f), last time finishing 9l behind to Perseus Way, who is 9lb worse off.
runners.comment: On a long losing run; three sound runs here this term, last time 9l 2nd to Perseus Way

runners.name: Haarar
runners.spotlight: Has won five of his 18 starts over hurdles, the latest at Carlisle in February (2m1f, good to soft); hasn't been in quite such good form since (fifth of six on the Flat last time).
runners.comment: 5-18 over hurdles, latest win at Carlisle in February; not in quite such good form since

runners.name: High Dancer
runners.spotlight: In the frame in four of his last five starts at up to 3m, last time finishing a head second in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Market Rasen (2m4f, good); this drop in trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Head second over 2m4f with headgear (retained) last time; drop to 2m a concern

runners.name: Winds Of Winter
runners.spotlight: Cartmel winner off 2lb lower last July (2m1f, good) who was a fair fifth in a mares' handicap over that C&D last time; not out of it.
runners.comment: Cartmel winner over 2m1f last July; fair fifth there last time; not out of it

races.race_name: Tynedale Function Suite Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Laffer Curve
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler who was a creditable second of four to a progressive rival in a mares' handicap at Worcester on chase debut this month (2m4f, good; first run after wind op; made a mistake four out, but rallied); one with a chance.
runners.comment: Maiden; sound chase debut after wind op when 2nd to a decent mare at Worcester; chance

runners.name: Ostend
runners.spotlight: Ex-Alastair Ralph; well beaten on all three starts following wind surgery last season but solid run on stable debut when second at Market Rasen on Friday (2m5f, good); interesting.
runners.comment: Ex-Alastair Ralph; best race for a while when 2nd on stable debut on Friday; interesting

runners.name: Moodofthemoment
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins, latest at Newcastle last October (2m4f, good); not fared as well since, but now back on last winning mark.
runners.comment: Four races wins, latest in October; below form since, but back on last winning mark

runners.name: West Lawn
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 20th attempt over fences when winning here in May (3m, good); decent fourth next time (3m, good); place chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark over fences here in May (3m good); good 4th here next time; place chance

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: Has done well since tongue-tie went on, winning his last three races, all of them on good ground here, two at this trip and the latest at 3m; has gone up 16lb (6lb higher than last time); contender.
runners.comment: In fine form since tongue-tie on, winning last 3 races, all on good here; up 16lb; chance

runners.name: Buddah Castle
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in March 2025 (3m, good); winning debut over hurdles at Sedgefield last November (2m4f, soft; four ran) but poor runs next two starts; may revive now chasing.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; won on hurdles debut in November, but poor efforts since; chase debut
runners.owner: T Alderson & Ales And Dales Syndicate

races.race_name: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Miss Kassiopi
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire when dropped back to 2m six weeks ago, her final start for Richard Bandey, but was in good form at about 2m4f beforehand, notably when making all at Hereford (good) in April; recently changed hands for £20,000 and now tackles a new trip on stable debut; interesting to see what Dan Skelton can do with her.
runners.comment: Won over 2m3f in April; now with Dan Skelton after recent £20,000 sale

runners.name: Robert d'Ores
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, most recently off 5lb higher in October (good to soft); well below best this year, though, and looked unenthusiastic when last of six over C&D (first run after wind operation) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner; looked unenthusiastic when last of six here this month, though

runners.name: Star Vantage
runners.spotlight: Belied 40-1 odds when fourth from out of the weights in quite valuable series final at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) in March but was well beaten when tried in a visor in April (2m4f) and is now 0-31 over hurdles; sports refitted cheekpieces here; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Well beaten when tried in a visor in April and now 0-31 over hurdles; others preferred

runners.name: Belladinotte
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) a year ago and doubled her tally at Southwell (3m, good) in April, after a break; returns here after respectable Southwell run 19 days ago and after a recent wind operation; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won over C&D a year ago and was also on target at Southwell in April; shortlisted

runners.name: Myfavouritesister
runners.spotlight: Didn't run as badly as eventual 36l defeat suggests when back over hurdles at Perth (3m, good) a fortnight ago but she's now 0-14 and is not the most obvious answer here.
runners.comment: Didn't run as badly as distance beaten suggests last time but she's now 0-14

runners.name: Jeteye
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely and was slightly disappointing when fourth here a fortnight ago but battled well for narrow C&D (good) win last month and is still on a workable mark; player if he settles better this time.
runners.comment: Raced too freely on latest outing but was a game C&D winner two starts ago

runners.name: Stylish Recruit
runners.spotlight: Not one to rely upon heavily nowadays but won gamely off 5lb lower here (2m4f, good) in April and his recent C&D second was also a good effort; could have a say.
runners.comment: Not one to rely upon nowadays but won here (2m4f) in April and recently ran well over C&D

runners.name: Luminaries
runners.spotlight: Gained all four hurdle wins on good ground during purple patch in spring 2025 (2m4f-3m; wore cheekpieces); out of form during the winter and again well beaten this month, after a break; revival needed.
runners.comment: Won four times in spring 2025 but has lost his way since

runners.name: Twoplacesatonetime
runners.spotlight: 1-13 over hurdles, the win gained over C&D (good) in 2024; never really got going during fairly light campaign last season and looked held before coming to grief before home turn here last month (race won by Jeteye); others have less to prove.
runners.comment: Never really got going last season and came to grief here last month

runners.name: Dartmhor
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles, having ended last season with some disappointing efforts; 3lb out of the weights here and needs April's wind operation to have made a big difference.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles and 3lb wrong here; needs April's wind op to have made a big difference

runners.name: Nitty Gritty
runners.spotlight: Second to a next-time-out winner at Kelso (2m5f, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month but has very mixed record for Mark Walford since the autumn and is not easy to predict on this stable debut; cheekpices removed.
runners.comment: Has mixed record for Mark Walford since the autumn; hard to predict on stable debut

runners.name: Lady Phoebe
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but ran quite well when fourth at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March and her subsequent 2m5f Flat win makes her of strong interest over this new trip.
runners.comment: 2m5f Flat winner in April; of strong interest over this new trip over hurdles

runners.name: Jimli's Cave
runners.spotlight: Irish raider; reverts to hurdling after running reasonably well in a recent Downpatrick chase (2m7f, good) but has a modest strike-rate (1-23) and is very inconsistent; refitted visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran okay over fences last week but is inconsistent; headgear switched

races.race_name: Fostering Changes Lives Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Pentonville
runners.spotlight: In cheekpieces the last twice; pushed the long odds-on favourite close on his handicap debut (1m2f, good to firm) and then won as he liked at Wolverhampton on Tuesday (8.6f); looks well in with the penalty and this 295,000gns yearling could easily rate much higher.
runners.comment: Won easily on Tuesday and this 295,000gns yearling could easily rate much higher
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Kodi Fire
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick last summer, over 7f here and then twice at 1m; not at the same level this season but realistically treated if he can turn things around in a new tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Realistically treated if he can turn things around in a new tongue-tie

runners.name: Palazzo Persico
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop who lost his way in Ireland but bounced back to form last Monday (1m2f, good to soft; fine on faster) when a clearcut winner on his debut for this yard; William Pearman rides again with a penalty to carry.
runners.comment: Carries a penalty for Monday's win but that was decisive on his debut for this yard

runners.name: Endowed
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f handicaps at Chepstow last June and went close in his hat-trick bid the following month (7f); two lesser efforts followed and he returns to action after 263 days on the sidelines.
runners.comment: Capable but below par last two runs and returns to action after 263 days on the sidelines

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 2-46, with the wins in Ireland during the autumn of 2023; not beaten far in his last two completed starts in 1m2f/1m3f handicaps; back at 1m here, he looks very vulnerable to this week's two winners.
runners.comment: Back at 1m here, he looks very vulnerable to this week's two winners

runners.name: Darvel
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner last year at a mile but slow starts have become a habit and was tailed off last time despite racing prominently.
runners.comment: Three wins last year at 1m but this season hasn't got off the ground at all

races.race_name: Brighton & Hove Fostering Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Nevasca Cinza
runners.spotlight: Two promising runs (6f, good and soft) for another yard as a 2yo; not at the same level in three runs for James Fanshawe and hopes appear to rest on the faster ground sparking something extra from him; cheekpieces tried last time swiftly dispensed with.
runners.comment: Not matched 2yo form in three runs for James Fanshawe; faster ground needs to help

runners.name: Court Alert
runners.spotlight: Second of 20 in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August his clear standout effort and a repeat would make him hard to beat; two heavy defeats have followed, including on his reappearance (7f, good) ten weeks ago; had wind surgery since and he commands respect back sprinting.
runners.comment: Last summer's Newmarket 2nd a standout effort; had wind op since low-key return; chance

runners.name: Ridger
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to winners Arizona Blaze (5f-6f including 2yo/AW/Group 1; RPR 121), Sailthisshipalone (7f 2yo/1m; 91) and Dreaming Spires (1m; 70); dam 1m2f AW winner (55); starts out at a realistic level and the betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Group 1 winner Arizona Blaze; starts out at realistic level; check betting

runners.name: Debrief
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns foal; well held in two novices (6f/7f) seven months apart; capable of better but perhaps once handicapping over further than this 6f.
runners.comment: Has hinted at ability in two novice runs (6f-7f, good ground); may need more of a test

runners.name: Sugar Sugar
runners.spotlight: Well-related filly but she looked a hard ride when finishing down the field at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) 11 days ago, pulling hard early and then hanging badly left from 2f out; while those errant ways need ironing out quickly, she did travel as though there was an engine there and it's not inconceivable that she will be a different proposition around a left-hand turn.
runners.comment: Looked a hard ride on debut but also showed clear signs of ability; could fare much better

runners.name: Landslide
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when well beaten in a 7f novice at Leicester in April; a hood and cheekpieces are now added but dropping back to 6f isn't sure to suit judged on breeding.
runners.comment: 80-1 and well beaten in a 7f novice in April; accessories added but drop to 6f a query

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Douglas Dc
runners.spotlight: Has poor 1-53 strike-rate, the win gained over hurdles in 2022, and was soundly beaten on both outings this year (11.4f/1m2f); might be perked up by the refitted blinkers, though, and Billy Loughnane is a good booking.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on both outings this year; might be perked up by refitted blinkers

runners.name: Power Of Prayer
runners.spotlight: Won in Ireland (1m4f, AW) in the autumn and made pleasing stable debut when running-on third here (1m2f, good; behind Night Bear) 12 days ago; back up in trip today; dangerous if that recent outing brought her on.
runners.comment: Made pleasing stable debut when running-on third here (1m2f) 12 days ago; 1m4f also suits

runners.name: Gearing's Point
runners.spotlight: Close second on turf return at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month and remains on a workable mark but her latest run was disappointing and it's nearly three years since her last win.
runners.comment: Second on last month's turf return but not in same form since; last win was in 2023

runners.name: Night Bear
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his C&D win just over two years ago but lost out only by a neck when clear second back here (1m2f, good) 12 days ago and is sure to be suited by this move back up in trip; high on the list.
runners.comment: Went close over 1m2f here 12 days ago and this longer trip probably suits him better

runners.name: Koko Blue
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; ran well when third (just behind Gearing's Point) on seasonal/stable debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month; has live claims if able to build on that performance here but she's not entirely straightforward and there are a few heavy defeats on her record.
runners.comment: Ran well on last month's seasonal/stable debut but is not one to rely upon

runners.name: Suitcase Smith
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three handicaps this season (1m-1m4f) and has now been unplaced all seven starts; needs to learn to settle better; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well beaten three handicaps this season (1m-1m4f) and has now been unplaced all seven runs

runners.name: Amber Honey
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Ed Dunlop with respectable fifth of 15 at Nottingham (8.3f, good; visored) in April but was always behind on recent stable debut (1m, AW; no headgear) and it's hard to know what to expect from her over this new trip; blinkers refitted.
runners.comment: Always behind over 1m on recent stable debut; tackles a new trip here

runners.name: Sarangpur
runners.spotlight: 0-6 when based in Ireland but made the frame in two slow-ground handicaps this spring (9.5f/10.5f); could have a say if suited by new trip-and-ground combination on stable debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Made the frame in two slow-ground Irish handicaps this spring; new trip for stable debut

runners.name: La Trinite
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who struggled in three middle-distance handicaps for Mike Murphy last summer; makes stable debut after 11-month absence (same owner); could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Absent since struggling in three handicaps for another stable last summer

races.race_name: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: First-time cheekpieces did the trick at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) four runs ago and was bang there in his next two races at 1m2f; the extended 1m3f perhaps stretched him last time; solid claims.
runners.comment: Running well before perhaps stretched by an extended 1m3f last time; solid chance

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: Three wins last summer included two over C&D but he needs to improve on recent efforts, the latest here.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer included two over C&D but he needs to improve on recent efforts

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Competing off this mark (handicap debut) asks more questions of him than his recent classified races yet he wasn't overly stretched in any of them, the latest over 1m2f at Lingfield on Thursday (good); might well make it five wins within the space of 11 days.
runners.comment: Racked up a four-timer in classified races and major chance in this first handicap

runners.name: Masqool
runners.spotlight: Good runs on the AW earlier this year but unconvincing since those and has another switch on the headgear front.
runners.comment: Good runs earlier this year but unconvincing since those; another change of headgear

runners.name: West Hill Rosie
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a bumper; showed a little more on her third Flat start when beaten under 8l at Bath (1m2f, good to firm), though was still only fifth in a field of seven; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed a little more on her third Flat start when beaten under 8l at Bath

runners.name: Dogged
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on turf but very quiet in two runs for this yard, the latest only last week; tries blinkers.
runners.comment: Five-time winner on turf but quiet in two runs for this yard, the latest only last week

races.race_name: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Gretna Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice last August, over C&D (good) and at Newmarket (good to firm); last three runs have been very underwhelming but on a handy mark if able to stage a revival.
runners.comment: C&D winner and she's on a handy mark if able to stage a revival
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Oj Lifestyle
runners.spotlight: Goes on any ground; all his handicap wins have been here over 1m (seems to stay 1m2f), the latest in October off this mark; down the field in both races this year but those were after lengthy breaks; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Goes very well here and this year's defeats have been after lengthy breaks

runners.name: Extraterrestrial
runners.spotlight: 0-8; did go very close on the AW (1m3f; new cheekpieces) in April but pretty much tailed off in his subsequent races and his freegoing tendencies remain evident.
runners.comment: 0-8; poor runs the last twice and his freegoing tendencies remain evident

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; modest overall strike-rate but not in recent weeks having won three of his last five races, all victories at Bath (1m-1m2f); still on a fair mark and good to soft ground was a feasible excuse at Chepstow last Monday.
runners.comment: Running well and good to soft ground was a feasible excuse at Chepstow last Monday

runners.name: Danehill Star
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner who is 0-10 on grass but placed in 1m handicaps at Bath in his last two races and returning to further looks a good thing.
runners.comment: Yet to win on turf but two good recent runs at Bath; goes further here and that's fine

runners.name: Meritocratic
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; gelded and in a first-time visor when a front-running third at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm); had no excuses in that 4.25l defeat but it's something to build on.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; gelded and in first-time visor when a front-running third at Windsor (1m2f)

runners.name: The Wicked Wolf
runners.spotlight: He raced freely last season but still won a 1m novice at Bath the final time (good to soft; unraced on quicker on turf); he's not unrealistically treated off this mark judged on what the second at Bath has achieved since, and being gelded and the addition of headgear might settle him down.
runners.comment: He raced freely last season but still won a 1m novice at Bath the final time

runners.name: Lenny's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner but an 8yo now and hasn't been remotely threatening in last four races.
runners.comment: Multiple winner but an 8yo now and hasn't been remotely threatening in last four races

runners.name: Virtue Chastity
runners.spotlight: Failed to show much in three AW maidens over 1m/1m2f during the winter; makes her turf/handicap debut after a lengthy break and the market needs checking.
runners.comment: Makes her turf/handicap debut after a lengthy break and the market needs checking

races.race_name: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Classified Stakes (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qual)

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt when justifying favouritism in a 7f classified event at Leicester (good ground; ridden by Billy Loughnane) 13 days ago; leading claims of following up.
runners.comment: 15th time lucky when winning a similar race at Leicester two weeks ago; strong contender

runners.name: Mister Sandman
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; track and ground no concern and he was running on well when second over 5.3f here 12 days ago; has been placed over 7f but others may see it out better.
runners.comment: 0-12 but threatening at this level & he's run well in both Brighton starts; 7f a slight ?

runners.name: Spirited Dancer
runners.spotlight: Disappointing since her early-season 2yo days; plummeted down the weights as a consequence and latest Bath second (5.7f, good), her first run at this level, was more encouraging; stamina for 7f has to be taken on trust though.
runners.comment: Back to form when 2nd at this level at Bath (5.7f, good) 16 days ago; stamina not assured

runners.name: Barnsnape Boy
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back handicaps at around 5f last summer, including here, and he showed 7f was in range when winning a similar C&D event 12 days ago, leading close home; more appealing than most.
runners.comment: Led late when winning a similar race over C&D 12 days ago; more appealing than most

runners.name: Skyolaire
runners.spotlight: Similar form in three handicap runs at Bath this year (1m-1m2f), showing no improvement for the addition of cheekpieces last time; drops in trip but likely vulnerable.
runners.comment: Similar form in three Bath handicaps over further this year; improvement required

runners.name: Embarked
runners.spotlight: Hanging to his left may have cost him dear when a close fourth behind Barnsnape Boy in a C&D classified event 12 days ago; each-way claims if keeping straighter.
runners.comment: Bold bid over C&D but hung his chance away in the closing stages; each-way shout

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Just one win to her name (5f, AW) from 35 starts and she has finished out the back on her last two starts; steps up to 7f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and stepping up in trip is not enough to tempt

runners.name: Imthequeenofsheba
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the drop into a classified race when seventh of ten at Wolverhampton (7f, AW; 16-1) on her seasonal/stable debut 19 days ago; too much to prove.
runners.comment: Seventh of ten at this level on her seasonal/stable debut 19 days ago; plenty to prove

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: Finished behind three of today's rivals in a similar race over C&D 12 days ago but she was keeping on nicely close home and remains capable of better; high on the list.
runners.comment: Finished behind three of these over C&D 12 days ago but shaped nicely; capable of better

runners.name: Almavillalobas
runners.spotlight: Poor ten-race maiden; hard to recommend, even at this level.
runners.comment: Poor ten-race maiden; hard to recommend, even at this level
runners.owner: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson

runners.name: My Mate Beattie
runners.spotlight: 0-24 and her record for this yard is modest; not the obvious answer.
runners.comment: 0-24 and her record for this yard is modest; not the obvious answer

runners.name: My Boy Jack
runners.spotlight: Finished third in this race last year but he isn't arriving in the same form this time around; easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year but not at best when last seen and others look stronger

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now Handicap

runners.name: Correspondence
runners.spotlight: Running well in turf handicaps this summer, following his success at Windsor (5f, good to firm; cheekpieces first time) with a good second (winner gone in again since) at Bath (5f, firm) two weeks later; up in grade but could well have more to offer.
runners.comment: Progressing in fast-ground sprint handicaps this year; unexposed in cheekpieces; chance

runners.name: The Flying Seagull
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he ran well on his return to turf at Windsor last month and then lost his chance at the start at Bath 16 days ago (blindfold off late); on a good mark and his rider takes off a handy 5lb; interesting with new blinkers replacing a visor.
runners.comment: Had a valid excuse last time; earlier Windsor fourth was promising; one to take seriously
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Hoodie Hoo
runners.spotlight: His sole success from 12 attempts came in a small-field 6f AW maiden last March; plenty of solid runs in handicaps (turf and AW) since and belatedly dropping to 5f, albeit an extended one, could give him a lift.
runners.comment: All runs at 6f; several good handicap runs and dropping in trip can help; contender
runners.owner: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff

runners.name: The Thames Boatman
runners.spotlight: Prolific AW winner; off the mark on turf with a last-gasp success at Goodwood (5f, good) last month; would have finished hot on the heels of Moulin Booj at Windsor last time but for meeting late interference; in the mix again.
runners.comment: Finally off the mark on turf at Goodwood last month; another good effort latest; e-w shout

runners.name: Rogue Bullet

runners.name: Chill Master

runners.comment: Drops into a Class 5 here but hasn't looked that progressive and the draw is a blow

runners.comment: Reserve; only win came on dirt and recent turf form doesn't bode well

runners.comment: 0-20 but 7f suits better than 6f and he's on a good mark based on best form

runners.name: Giddy Up

runners.comment: Only ninth over C&D in January and also opposable on what he's shown in trials

runners.comment: All his runs have been respectable and he looks ready for this step up to 6f

runners.comment: Showed nothing more than minor promise on first two starts; up in trip; headgear on

runners.name: Happy Smile
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only two starts and kept on for respectable fifth over 6f here three weeks ago; drawn quite wide for first attempt at 7f but probably still has potential.

runners.comment: Made all over C&D last month; 9lb higher and drawn wide today but Purton rides again

runners.name: Illuminous

runners.spotlight: Won over 1m1f at Happy Valley in December and was placed on consecutive appearances here (1m/1m1f) in March/April but his form has dipped lately; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Placed here twice in March/April but form has dipped lately
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent this season but led close home to score over 1m here three weeks ago; still on a good mark after recent 5lb rise and this longer trip suits him better; dangerous if on another going day.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season but won over 1m here recently and this trip suits him better
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Mighty Strength
runners.spotlight: Came clear to win readily over 1m1f here last month but is not always easy to predict and a recent 9lb rise asks quite a lot of him.
runners.comment: Ready course winner last month but recent 9lb rise asks quite a lot of him
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Li Ming On, Joyce Li Lok Yan & Henry Li Lok Him

runners.name: Winning Dragon
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime but has not fired on any of three starts during light campaign this season; revival needed.
runners.comment: Smart in his prime but has not fire on any of three starts during light campaign this term
runners.owner: Alexander Yeung Kwok Kai, Edward Yeung Et Al

runners.name: Noisy Boy
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fourth at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month but was soundly beaten over that C&D 11 days ago and is patently not the force of old; others have much more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Fourth at Happy Valley two starts ago but this regressive 5yo is on a long losing run
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Ricky Leung Ghin Ming

runners.name: Nucleozor
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six Hong Kong starts but hasn't been with current stable long and his keeping-on fifth here (1m) three weeks ago was quite encouraging; could have a say if this new trip suits.
runners.comment: Kept on well for fifth over 1m here last month; tries a new trip today

runners.name: Call Me Magnifique
runners.spotlight: 3-9 when trained in Australia but struggled on first two Hong Kong starts (1m/1m1f).
runners.comment: 3-9 when trained in Australia but struggled on first two Hong Kong starts (1m/1m1f)
runners.owner: 25/26 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Shamus Storm
runners.spotlight: 2-14 in Hong Kong, the latter win gained by a short head over 1m1f here in March; not beaten far when fifth over that trip at Happy Valley recently and promises to be suited by this extra furlong; high on the list.
runners.comment: Won over 1m1f here in March and promises to be suited by this extra furlong; high on list
runners.owner: Lily Chan Li Lei & Marc Chan

runners.name: Jumbo Steps
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only three runs in Hong Kong and the last two (7f/1m) were quite encouraging; needs this new trip to unlock improvement, though.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only three runs in Hong Kong but needs improvement over this new trip

runners.name: Enthusium
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong at fourth attempt, with narrow C&D success on soft ground last month; disappointed when dropped back to 1m1f three weeks later but may yet build upon his winning performance.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month, on his fourth HK run; well held since but still enters calculations

runners.name: Fallon
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when scoring at Happy Valley (1m1f) in March but recent efforts have been disappointing and he's still on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have been disappointing and he's still on a tough mark

runners.name: Loch Tay
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m dirt winner here this season; made the frame off today's mark on that surface last month but needs to prove he can match that form on turf.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner this season; needs to prove he can match that form back on turf

races.race_name: Premier Plate (Group 3) (Handicap) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Romantic Thor
runners.spotlight: Made all in 1m4f Group 3 handicap here last month and was very respectable fourth in Group 1 over that trip three weeks later but drops back in trip and contests this race from 8lb out of the weights; on balance, others appeal more.
runners.comment: Made all in 1m4f Group 3 here last month; back in trip and out of the weights today
runners.owner: Peter Lau Pak Fai & Suki Tang Xianfang

runners.name: Ka Ying Generation
runners.spotlight: Placed in two C&D handicaps in March/April and faced very tough assignment in 1m4f Group 1 on latest outing but looks up against it again today from 10lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Placed in two C&D handicaps this year but is 10lb out of the weights today

runners.name: Beauty Joy
runners.spotlight: Four-time Group winner, most recently in this race last year; long in the tooth and on the downgrade now but lost out only narrowly to Light Years Charm off much-reduced mark in 1m Group 3 here three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 and went close off much reduced mark here last month
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Nautical Force
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; managed only fourth of seven when favourite for 1m4f Group 3 handicap here in early May and is effectively 8lb higher from out of the weights today but won Class 2 C&D handicap on local debut in April and remains unexposed in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Held when favourite last time (1m4f) but won over C&D on Hong Kong debut in April

runners.name: Gentlemen Legacy
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who won consecutive 1m2f races here in February/March and took another step forward when second to Romantic Thor in 1m4f Group 3 handicap last month; this drop back in trip is probably not ideal but his recent Group 1 defeat can be overlooked; one to consider.
runners.comment: Can be excused recent Group 1 defeat and was making good progress beforehand

runners.name: Chancheng Glory
runners.spotlight: Placed in 1m2f Group 1 last season but not really firing in all cylinders this term, and managed only eighth of nine in recent 1m Group 3 handicap won by Light Years Charm.
runners.comment: Below best here this season, managing only eighth of nine in 1m Group 3 here last month

runners.name: Californiatotality
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with 37-1 third to Silvery Breeze in Class 2 handicap at Happy Valley this month and remains on the same workable mark; contender if in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Returned to form with recent Happy Valley third and remains on same workable mark

runners.name: Silvery Breeze
runners.spotlight: Improving 5yo who won a bit more cosily than narrow margin suggests when returned to 1m1f for Class 2 handicap at Happy Valley 18 days ago; still unexposed over this trip; major player in first Group race.
runners.comment: Won a bit more cosily than narrow margin suggests at Happy Valley this month; big player

runners.name: Light Years Charm
runners.spotlight: Evidently suited by return to 1m when clinging on gamely to fend off Beauty Joy in 1m Group 3 here three weeks ago; tries a new trip today; recent 5lb rise might leave him vulnerable.
runners.comment: Held on gamely to win 1m Group 3 here last month; 5lb rise might leave him vulnerable

runners.name: Sunlight Power
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when close second in 1m Group 2 in November and has held his own at Group 1 level since but his overall record is rather mixed and others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Can hold his own at Group 1 level but his overall record is rather mixed

runners.name: Cap Ferrat
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Hong Kong Derby over 1m2f and bettered that form when close second in 1m Group 1 here in April; needs to rebound from a disappointing 1m4f Group 1 last month but could be too good for this field if all goes to plan.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but went close in 1m Group 1 here two runs ago; the class act

races.race_name: Sapphire Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when keeping-on sixth over C&D last month and remains on a good mark but has not been firing on all cylinders this year and has awkward draw today; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders this year and has awkward draw today; others preferred
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Chicken Dinner
runners.spotlight: Launched Hong Kong career with two low-key runs but made good headway from the rear and belied 59-1 odds when second over C&D last month; Zac Purton sidles in for the ride today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Much-improved form when second over C&D last month; Zac Purton takes the ride today

runners.name: Uranus Star
runners.spotlight: Now back on same mark as for 1m win at Happy Valley in November but recent 7f efforts have been below par and this drop to 6f is not an obvious move.
runners.comment: Recent 7f efforts have been below par and this drop to 6f is not an obvious move

runners.name: Packing King
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo is 2-2 over C&D for new stable; upped in grade today and Zac Purton can't do the weight but his latest win was gained in good style and Matthew Poon is an able deputy.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo is 2-2 over C&D for new stable; up in grade; still respected

runners.name: Golden Champ
runners.spotlight: Reserve; four-time winner when based in New Zealand but launched Hong Kong career with five low-key runs (6f-1m); difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with five low-key runs (6f-1m); difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Crossborderpegasus
runners.spotlight: Often slowly away but has consistent and steadily progressive profile; kept on well for second off today's mark over C&D three weeks ago and is likely to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Consistent and steadily progressive 4yo; second over C&D three weeks ago; big player

runners.name: Majestic Valour
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over C&D in Class 4; has found things a bit tougher since upped to this grade for last two runs but is well drawn today and may yet have a bigger run in him.
runners.comment: 2-3 in Class 4; finding things a bit tougher in this grade but has good draw today
runners.owner: Richard Yue Wah Chuk & David Pao

runners.name: Harmony N Blessed
runners.spotlight: Regressive 9yo; suggested he might be ready to capitalise upon some help from the handicapper when second at Happy Valley in April but two down-the-field runs have followed and he now has it all to prove again.
runners.comment: Regressive 9yo who finished down the field on his last two outings
runners.owner: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al

runners.name: Ten Loves
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia in September (6f) but was always behind when a big-priced outsider for recent Hong Kong debut (5f).
runners.comment: Always behind when a big-priced outsider for recent Hong Kong debut (5f)

runners.name: Quantum Cloud
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ex-Australian 3yo; good start to Hong Kong career when fourth over C&D in February but raced too freely and was below that form four weeks later; returns from 98-day break.
runners.comment: Good fourth over C&D on HK debut in February but next run was a backward step

runners.name: Tang Heart
runners.spotlight: 2-9 when trained in Australia; endured a wide trip before keeping on for third in competitive C&D contest on Hong Kong debut three weeks ago; contender if able to build on that performance.
runners.comment: Endured wide trip before keeping on for third in competitive C&D race on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Flying Hunter
runners.spotlight: Towards rear throughout when big-priced outsider for first three Hong Kong starts (all 6f); cannot be recommended from stall 14.
runners.comment: Towards rear throughout when big-priced outsider for first three Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Titan Pegasus
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a 2yo in Australia, the latter win gained in 5.5f Group 3; well down the field on Hong Kong debut four weeks ago but suffered interference that day and can be given another chance.
runners.comment: Won Group 3 in Australia; suffered interference on HK debut, can be given another chance

runners.name: Jin Sheng
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden in Australia in November, on his second start; makes Hong Kong debut after seven-month absence but can't grumble about his draw; market may guide.
runners.comment: Won 6f maiden in Australia in November, on his second start; makes Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Real Time
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f when based in New Zealand but was well beaten on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/7f).
runners.comment: Well beaten on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/7f)

runners.name: Love Espresso
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in 6f maiden handicap in Australia in December; looks an interesting recruit to the Hong Kong scene but has wide draw for local debut.
runners.comment: 6f winner on debut in Australia in December; has awkward draw for Hong Kong debut

races.race_name: Amethyst Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Almighty Lightning
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who won over 5f on debut here in February and bounced back quickly from a duff effort when second over 6f here last month; poorly drawn for first attempt at 7f but still makes appeal.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo; went close over 6f here last month; poorly drawn but still makes appeal

runners.name: New Future Folks
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 6f here in early part of this season but out of form over 6f/7f in recent months; favourable draw not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Out of form in recent months; favourable draw not enough to tempt

runners.name: All's Well
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in this higher grade since Class 4 C&D win in February and may still be on the upgrade; one to consider.
runners.comment: Placed twice in this higher grade since Class 4 C&D win in February; considered
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Leung Wai Zen

runners.name: King Equine
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 3yo who bettered his inauspicious Hong Kong debut when 82-1 third over C&D a fortnight ago; drawn wider today but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Bettered inauspicious HK debut when big-odds third over C&D last month

runners.name: Monarch County
runners.spotlight: Posted career-best performance when coming from off the pace to win easily over C&D last month and is now 3-17; 9lb higher and back up in grade today, though.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace for easy C&D win last month; back up in grade off 9lb higher
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo, Alex Fan Chen Yen Et Al

runners.name: Winning Gold
runners.spotlight: Began this year with three down-the-field runs over 1m but returned from three-month break with close second over C&D three weeks ago and remains well handicapped on older form; will be dangerous if in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Turned things around with close second over C&D last month; still well handicapped

runners.name: Everyone's Star
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when midfield over C&D last month but has been placed four times over C&D this season and his current mark is workable; contender if back on song.
runners.comment: Not at best last month but placed four times over C&D this season; a possible

runners.name: Prime Success
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a 2yo in Britain, the latter win gained in 1m nursery handicap in October; makes Hong Kong debut after eight-month absence and is entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: 2-3 as a 2yo in Britain; makes Hong Kong debut after eight-month absence

runners.name: Red Maestro
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in Ireland in early 2025 (1m, AW) and has performed well on all three Hong Kong starts this year (6f-7f); Zac Purton takes the ride today; likely contender.
runners.comment: Has made solid start to HK career and Zac Purton takes the ride today
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

runners.name: Riding Together
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on both outings last month (7f/1m) and is better drawn today but remains 5lb higher than for the second of this season's two C&D wins and might find others better handicapped.
runners.comment: In the frame twice last month (7f/1m) but may still be a shade too high in the weights

runners.name: Galactic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over 6f in Hong Kong and looked suited by step up in trip when keeping-on fourth over C&D last month; might still have more to offer over this trip; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth over C&D when upped to this trip last month; each-way claims
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Lam Ngai And Emily Lam Huen

runners.name: Glorious Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; made low-key Hong Kong debut when midfield over 6f here last month and is probably one for a bit further down the line.
runners.comment: Made low-key Hong Kong debut last month; probably one for further down the line

runners.name: Without Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Reserve; well beaten on both Hong Kong starts; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both Hong Kong starts; can't be recommended

runners.name: Seward
runners.spotlight: Signed off in Ireland with two 7f wins in April 2025 but has been soundly beaten on both Hong Kong starts this season (7f/1m).
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m)
runners.owner: David Philip Boehm & Anthony Cheung Hin Shun

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Reserve; smart 2yo when based in Ireland but a down-the-field outsider on all six Hong Kong starts (5f-7f).
runners.comment: Smart 2yo when based in Ireland but struggling to make an impact in Hong Kong

runners.name: Cool Boy
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who won three in a row over 6f here in December-February and has posted some good efforts from tough draws since; big player if this this extra furlong suits.
runners.comment: Progressive over 6f this season; big player if this extra furlong suits
runners.owner: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut

races.race_name: Prenio Oaks d'Italia Tattersalls (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Regina Di Spade
runners.owner: Scuderia Giacobbe Ferruccio E C Sas
